message:
feat(unistd): add flopen + flopenat (libbsd open-with-lock) (bd-qrq1q)

FreeBSD/libbsd flopen(path, flags, [mode]) atomically opens a
file AND acquires an advisory file-level flock. Companion
flopenat(dirfd, path, flags, [mode]) is the dirfd-relative
variant. Used by daemons and daemon-managers (rc.d, supervisord,
systemd unit helpers) to enforce single-instance pid files.

* O_SHLOCK (libbsd 0x10) and O_EXLOCK (libbsd 0x20) are stripped
  from the flags before delegating to open()/openat() since Linux
  doesn't recognize those bit positions.
* Lock kind: O_EXLOCK → LOCK_EX, O_SHLOCK alone → LOCK_SH,
  default → LOCK_EX.
* O_NONBLOCK in flags also OR's LOCK_NB into the flock op so the
  lock acquisition doesn't block.
* On lock failure the fd is closed before -1 is returned (errno
  preserved across the close).
* NULL path → -1 with errno=EFAULT (matches glibc behavior).

Both shims use a shared `flopen_with_dirfd` helper that takes the
open-call closure so flopen and flopenat share the same lock-and-
errno-preservation logic.

8 abi integration tests (real filesystem on /tmp): create with
default lock, O_EXLOCK on existing file, two simultaneous shared
locks succeed, non-blocking exclusive lock fails with EAGAIN/
EWOULDBLOCK when another holds the lock, nonexistent path →
ENOENT, NULL path → EFAULT, flopenat with AT_FDCWD, flopenat NULL
guard. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
